mission.title: kong: db-less kong get empty reply when targets numbers > 3 in post json
mission.description: db-less kong get empty reply when targets numbers > 3 in post json . Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kong troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kong deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
I use kong and kubernetes ingress controller in arm64v8 environment, but I encountered a strange problem, kubernetes-ingress-controller failed to send post to kong admin api.

steps.title: Apply the fix for db-less kong get empty reply when targets numbers > 3 in…
steps.description:
> Is there any way to disable luajit in arm64? Maybe this make us easy to debug problem.

This issue is a bug in LuaJIT's interpreter, not in the JIT itself.  specifically, it's in the bytecode instruction used to set a table element within a "spliced in" built-in function, like table.move.  the

steps.title: Confirm db-less kong get empty reply when targets numbers… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> Is there any way to disable luajit in arm64? Maybe this make us easy to debug problem.

This issue is a bug in LuaJIT's interpreter, not in the JIT itself.  specifically, it's in the bytecode instruction used to set a table element within a "spliced in" built-in function, like table.move.  the best solution is not to use table.move until it's fixed.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
